content: EC-VERIFY-FIRST-001: before touching orchestrator-role/SKILL.md, read its live on-disk content and check .pmcro/trails/cto/ for prior trails against this same file, rather than assuming this is a fresh task. Success condition: confirmed current state, not a prior session's self-report.

Found sealed trail cto/67f24fd5-8b75-4a5f-b8f8-ba9393ff347d, disposition accept, which already: replaced the Law-2 relative chain with '<repo_path>/.agents/rules/02-pmcro-cycle-discipline.md', renamed 'Invocation Contract' to 'Usage (System Contract)', and converted the 'Reference Files' section to full repo-relative paths. Its reflect.jsonl logged an open (non-blocking) hypothesis: inline '../../references/...' and '../../scripts/' mentions inside 'The Cycle' steps 1-2 and the 'Multi-Turn Evolution Work' section were left untouched, out of that cycle's narrower scope. This cycle's true_intent ('replace all relative ../../ paths') reads as picking up that open item. Success condition: this interpretation stated explicitly, not silently assumed.

Interpretive note, flagged rather than silently resolved: true_intent's literal wording ('replace ... with <repo_path>/.agents/rules/') would misroute the remaining inline references -- they point at this same package's own references/ and scripts/ subfolders, not at Colony Law files, and .agents/rules/ contains only the four Law files. Plan is to convert the remaining inline '../../references/*.md' and '../../scripts/' mentions to the same full repo-relative style already used in the 'Reference Files' section (catalog/Platform/PMCR-O/skills/orchestrator/references/*.md and .../scripts/new_trail_id.py), for internal consistency, and to raise the literal-vs-intended discrepancy to the human rather than either blindly complying or silently overriding. This does NOT touch the Law-2 reference already fixed. Success condition: every remaining '../../' instance in the file accounted for, each with a stable full path.

Re-read the full file after edits to confirm zero remaining '../../' instances and no other prose altered (EC-010: grep-verify across the whole file, not a partial pass). Success condition: search for the literal substring '../../ ' in the post-edit file returns no matches.

Design catalog/Platform/PMCR-O/skills/orchestrator/commands/brief-session.md as a new command, matching the existing commands/ style (replay-trail.md, seal-trail.md): frontmatter description + usage line, parameter block, then behavior. It must (a) simulate a 'Round Table' -- each of the 10 registered C-Suite domains (from catalog/skills.json) giving a short persona-voiced status line for session hydration, sourced from that domain's own SKILL.md scope plus any open items visible in .pmcro/trails/<domain>/ -- and (b) write/update session-persistent memory following a named 'Memory Bank' convention. Success condition: file created, matches sibling commands' frontmatter/style, and names its Memory Bank location explicitly rather than leaving it implicit.

No existing 'Memory Bank' convention exists anywhere in this repo (confirmed via repo-wide filename search before planning this step -- zero matches for '*emory*'). Define it fresh, following the widely-used Cline/Roo-Code Memory Bank pattern (chosen because this Colony's own history traces through the earlier pmcro-cline project, per user's prior sessions): a small set of core markdown files -- projectbrief.md, activeContext.md, systemPatterns.md, techContext.md, progress.md -- stored under '.agents/memory-bank/' (sibling to the existing .agents/rules/ and .agents/plugins/, following this repo's own established .agents/ layering convention from COLONY.md). brief-session.md reads these at session start (if present) and updates activeContext.md + progress.md after the Round Table, rather than inventing a new location or format ad hoc. Success condition: convention named explicitly, file set enumerated, location justified by an existing repo pattern rather than an arbitrary choice.

This cycle writes only to catalog/ (orchestrator-role/SKILL.md edit, new brief-session.md command) and does not touch catalog/skills.json or either marketplace.json twin, rename/remove an existing entry, or git commit/push. Per hil-gating.md this still falls in the 'editing a Platform package's own files' zone; orchestrator's step 4 will decide seal disposition based on whether the human's dispatch here counts as the same explicit pre-authorization pattern that let trail 67f24fd5 seal accept. Flag for checker/orchestrator rather than assuming accept.
